description_long: This is a tall, double-handled ceramic vase with a rounded body and a narrow, cylindrical neck. The surface is predominantly white with blue painted motifs, including abstract floral or foliate designs, and some areas of brownish-red accent. The handles are robust and curve outward from the neck to the shoulder of the vase, each decorated with blue striping. Visible cracks and repairs suggest the vase has been restored, with some discoloration and patching evident on the neck and body. The base is slightly raised and unglazed, showing a natural ceramic color. The overall appearance is that of a traditional, possibly antique, decorative vase suitable for display in a public or semi-public urban setting.
description: A tall, white ceramic vase with blue painted motifs, double curved handles, and visible restoration marks. The body is rounded with a narrow neck and decorative striping on the handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing both curved handles, blue floral motifs, and visible restoration on the neck.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one handle and the curvature of the body, with visible cracks and repairs.
description_view_2: Rear view with both handles visible, blue motifs, and less prominent restoration marks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other handle, blue striping, and additional surface cracks.
